title: Missacc Non-Amazon Scrape - 2026-05-17

metadata.reason: Desktop thread full transcript export was not available; this file captures the user request, scrape actions, validation, outputs, and wrap-up steps for durable project memory.

next_steps: Use the Missacc amazon-schema CSV as the Step 1 raw image-row output.
Use the JSONL when actual text-only review records or review audit context are needed.
Remote Git, S3, and codex_chat_transcripts have been updated for this Missacc scrape.

messages.text: Requested scraping missacc.com as a first-time non-Amazon source for FWM. Explicitly asked to read new-scrape-rules.md, verify missacc_com had no existing local output/script/doc/claim, create a claim, scrape every public product on missacc.com, extract actual review records and customer-uploaded images with review text, rating/date, reviewer name, ordered size, measurement/body comments, product metadata and product URLs, exhaust paginated media responsibly, and stop on 429/captcha/WAF/auth challenges.

Read /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Repo/new-scrape-rules.md. Searched FWM_Repo and FWM_Data for missacc, missacc_com, and missacc.com. Found only a prior scrape_triage_plan.md mention naming missacc_com as a bridesmaid dress target; no existing output directory, scraper script, or claim was found.
Created /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Repo/_active_scrape_claims/missacc_com.claim recording the duplicate check, the prior triage mention, and the full scrape scope.

Discovered Missacc is a Nuxt storefront, not Shopify. robots.txt points to https://www.missacc.com/marketing/dress_missacc_us_sitemap_us.xml, whose product sitemap contained 10,318 public product URLs. Product pages and frontend chunks exposed anonymous public review endpoints under /rest/v1/review/list and /rest/v1/review/images.

Implemented /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Repo/data-pipelines/non-amazon/scripts/step_1_raw_scrape/scrape_missacc_reviews.py. The scraper uses the public product sitemap, anonymous Nuxt review endpoints, decoded anonymous token headers, product-level review pagination for every discovered SKU, media-feed pagination, JSONL capture for all actual review records, and CSV image rows matching the FWM Step 1 schema.

Ran a smoke pass against five products. It verified product-level review pagination and aggregate media pagination. Patched the media feed seed SKU behavior after discovering Missacc rejects a blank spuCode.

Ran the full scrape. A first attempt stopped locally around product 2,750 due to a UnicodeEncodeError from a hidden non-ASCII character in a product URL used as a Referer header. This was not a Missacc 429/captcha/WAF/auth block. Patched URL/header IRI-to-URI encoding and reran successfully.
Completed the full scrape cleanly: 10,318 products discovered and scanned; 21,106 product-level review pages scanned; 19 media pages scanned; no 429, captcha, WAF, or auth challenge; no stop reason.

Wrote outputs under /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/missacc_com. The canonical CSV is missacc_com_reviews_matching_amazon_schema.csv, the summary is missacc_com_reviews_matching_amazon_schema_summary.json, all actual review records are preserved in missacc_com_review_records.jsonl, and product coverage is preserved in missacc_com_public_catalog_snapshot.json.

Validated output counts and numeric fields. Final metrics: 69,086 actual review records, 11,501 image rows, 68,914 distinct reviews, 11,393 distinct images, 1,668 distinct product URLs, 11,501 rows with product URL, 11,501 rows with customer image, 2,786 rows with ordered size, 117 rows with any measurement, and 63 Supabase-qualified rows. Numeric normalized fields were checked and fixed to be numeric or blank.
Answered that Missacc produced 63 Supabase-qualified rows.
Asked to update the remote Git, S3, and the transcripts table.

Prepared this focused transcript for upload to the Supabase codex_chat_transcripts table, planned to sync the Missacc output data to S3, and planned to commit/push only the Missacc-scoped repo changes while avoiding unrelated dirty worktree files from other active scrape threads.

Confirmed remote Git is current: local HEAD and origin/main both resolve to 79a919895de6f5098604b3120f7ce1e3abcfcd8b, whose subject is Update Missacc scrape completion timestamp. A direct git push reported Everything up-to-date, though the sandbox could not refresh the local origin/main ref lock.

Synced /Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data to s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger using the default AWS profile available on this Mac. Verified the S3 Missacc prefix contains the catalog snapshot, review_records JSONL, amazon-schema CSV, summary JSON, and legacy intake-schema files. The sync uploaded the completed Missacc claim to the S3 _claims prefix.

Uploaded this transcript to Supabase table codex_chat_transcripts. First upload succeeded with chat_key codex-missacc-non-amazon-scrape-2026-05-17-756c532a9d7609e8 and message_count 14; the transcript was then refreshed to include the completed git/S3/transcripts update.
